title: Get a Contributions Payment Certificate (self-employed)
description: Self-employed people can ask for a certificate showing the NIS contributions they have paid.
summary: If you are self-employed in Barbados, you pay your own National Insurance contributions. Sometimes you need formal proof that you have done so – for example, when applying for a loan, a visa or filing your tax return.
The Contributions Payment Certificate is that proof. It is an official statement from the National Insurance Office showing what you have paid, and for which periods.

On the form, you tell the Office your details, the period the certificate should cover and what you need it for. The wording on the certificate is sometimes tailored to the purpose, so it helps to be specific.
Bring any past payment receipts you have. They make it easier for the Office to match your records and avoid delays.

items: your National Insurance Number and National Registration Number
the period the certificate should cover
what you need it for (so the right wording is used)

items.name: Past NIS payment receipts
items.note: If you have them, to help us match your records.

how_long: Certificates are usually ready within 10 working days.

options.heading: In person or by post
content: Take or send the completed Contributions Payment Certificate form to the National Insurance Office.

assumptions: ‘Certificates are usually ready within 10 working days’ is an estimate.
